text: Wandering these dimly lit spaces, illuminated only by emergency lights, I feel a sense of isolation and uncertainty. The silence amplifies my own thoughts—a rare environment that urges reflection, yet feels stifling, as if I am intruding on a world paused in tension. The cold echoes of industrial architecture and the faint traces of purpose on the walls hint at innovation and activity that now lie dormant. I am unsettled, my curiosity piqued but tinged with the unease of being somewhere I perhaps should not be.
